item.title: Asian Stocks Set for Gains as Oil Holds Losses: Markets Wrap
item.description: Asian stocks were set for a positive open after oil retreated on optimism about progress in peace talks between the US and Iran, offsetting weakness in Wall Street where drops in several tech giants weighed on benchmarks.

item.title: Oil Steadies as Iran Sales Waiver Signals Peace Talks Progress
item.description: Oil steadied as traders weighed early progress in peace talks over the Iran war, which included a US waiver allowing some sales of barrels from the Islamic Republic.

item.title: Fed’s Goolsbee Says Too-High Inflation Is ‘Going the Wrong Way’
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago President Austan Goolsbee said he remains concerned about inflation and questioned whether all the factors driving prices up are temporary.

item.title: Oracle Cut 21,000 Employees in 2026, Says AI Replaced Some Jobs
item.description: Oracle Corp. reduced its workforce by 21,000 employees in the past 12 months, a wider scale than previously known, including those whose jobs were eliminated by the use of artificial intelligence.

item.title: Avis Set for $650 Million Pentwater Payout After Short Squeeze
item.description: Avis Budget Group Inc. is set to receive $650 million in cash as part of a settlement agreement with Pentwater Capital Management to resolve a lawsuit regarding short-swing profits, according to a filing.

item.title: Vimeo Parent Bending Spoons, Backers Seek $1.62 Billion in IPO
item.description: Bending Spoons SpA, which acquires struggling software businesses, and some of its backers are seeking to raise as much as $1.62 billion in an initial public offering.

item.title: Schick Razor Maker Is Said to Reject Unsolicited Takeover Offer
item.description: Edgewell Personal Care Co., which makes Schick razors, has rejected an unsolicited takeover offer from private equity firm Yellow Wood Partners, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Qualcomm Nears Deal for AI Chip Startup Modular
item.description: Qualcomm Inc. is in advanced talks to acquire Modular Inc. in a transaction valuing the artificial intelligence chip startup at about $4 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Hedge Funds Piled Into Bearish Oil Bets Ahead of US-Iran MOU
item.description: Hedge funds boosted bearish bets on US crude to the most in nearly five months on expectations that a preliminary peace deal between Washington and Tehran will boost Middle East oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: 【今朝の5本】仕事を始める前に読んでおきたい厳選ニュース
item.description: 円が乱高下、イラン原油販売容認、急転直下の退場、マエストロ逝く、AI投資加速へ

item.title: Sony Plans Its First Dollar-Bond Sale in Nearly Three Decades
item.description: Sony Group Corp. was still marketing the original PlayStation when it last tapped the US investment-grade bond market. Almost three decades later, it’s readying a comeback.

item.title: CME Trading Disrupted as Platform Faces ‘Disconnects’
item.description: Trading on the world’s largest derivatives exchange has been disrupted as the platform experiences “disconnects,” CME Group told customers midday Monday, according to an email seen by Bloomberg News.

item.title: Venezuelan Bonds Tumble as Investors Brace for Debt Assessment
item.description: Venezuelan bonds tumbled to the lowest level in two months as investors braced for a highly anticipated review of the nation’s debt burden and economic prospects.

item.title: War, Inflation Can’t Stop ETFs’ Trillion-Dollar Inflow Boom
item.description: Exchange-traded funds are attracting cash at a record-breaking trillion-dollar pace despite persistent inflation, war in the Middle East and bouts of market volatility — a testament to American households’ enduring buy-and-hold conviction.

item.title: Easing of US Oil Sanctions Opens Brief Window for Iran Imports
item.description: The 60-day reprieve of US sanctions on Iranian oil, under an interim peace deal, has reopened the US market to the Middle Eastern country’s crude for the first time in 35 years.

item.title: Valve Prices Steam Machine Video Game Console Above $1,000, Citing Component Shortage
item.description: Valve Corp. announced pricing and availability details for the Steam Machine, a cube-shaped video game console that has seen its release pushed back several months due to pricing volatility around memory, solid-state drives and other components.

item.title: Iran Shipped 30 Million Barrels of Oil in Week Before US Waiver
item.description: Iranian crude is flooding onto the global market again, just as the US waives sanctions on the cargoes, permitting far more countries to buy them.

item.title: Greenspan’s Legacy: From Irrational Exuberance to 2008 Crisis
item.description: Tributes to former Federal Reserve Chairman Alan Greenspan poured in after the news of his death early Monday, many from former colleagues and market players who had a front-row seat during his 18-year tenure atop the US central bank.

item.title: Psychedelic Drugmaker Definium Surges on Depression Drug Success
item.description: Definium Therapeutics Inc. shares rally as much as 60% Monday after late-stage trial data showed “groundbreaking” results in the psychedelic drug developer’s experimental treatment for major depressive disorders.

item.title: Lagarde Says China Should Be Part of Any Talks on FX Imbalances
item.description: Any Group of Seven talks on currency valuations need to include China, according to European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde.

item.title: Kids’ Online Safety Bill Deal Struck by House GOP, Democrats
item.description: House lawmakers have reached a bipartisan deal on a legislative package intended to strengthen online safety and privacy protections for children and teenagers—a key sign of progress on a major policy priority.

item.title: Citadel Securities Says Warsh Could Stabilize Long US Bonds
item.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh’s commitment to lowering inflation bolsters the Fed’s credibility, supporting long-dated Treasury yields and a lower term-premium, according to Citadel Securities.

item.title: Mauritius Freezes Pension Means-Test After Backlash on Budget
item.description: Mauritius suspended plans to means-test state pensions after backlash over proposals unveiled in Friday’s budget, complicating its efforts to rein in its fiscal deficit.

item.title: Los Angeles Warehouse Fire Smoke Chokes Southern California for Fifth Day
item.description: A fire at a cold storage warehouse near downtown Los Angeles continued pumping fumes across Southern California five days after it ignited, prompting emergency declarations as air quality reached unhealthy levels.

item.title: Tech Rally Is Seen Having More Runway as AI Spending Gains Speed
item.description: Technology stocks will extend their rally for at least another couple of quarters as artificial intelligence infrastructure spending accelerates beyond the pace of the past two years, Columbia Threadneedle Investments’ Tiffany Wade said.

item.title: Gas Stations Accused of Using AI to Inflate Prices in California
item.description: A group of California consumers claimed in a lawsuit that gas station owners including Walmart Inc., Marathon Petroleum Corp., BP Plc and 7-Eleven Inc. are using artificial intelligence to illegally manipulate pump prices in the state that already has the highest rates in the US.

item.title: Centrica CEO Eyes US for More LNG Deals and Trading Expansion
item.description: The UK’s Centrica Plc plans to buy more US liquefied natural gas, as it sees the fuel remaining a key part of the energy system for decades even as global geopolitical tensions highlight the need to diversify supplies.

item.title: RWE to Sell €4.1 Billion in Shares to Boost Amprion Stake
item.description: German energy company RWE AG plans to raise about €4.1 billion ($4.7 billion) selling shares in order to increase its stake in one of the country’s transmission system operators — a significant step that marks the company’s increasing shift toward regulated infrastructure assets.

item.title: A Blockbuster Summer for Debt Issuance
item.description: Corporate bond bonanza.

item.title: Alphabet Shares Drop After Second AI Star Departs for Rival
item.description: Alphabet Inc. shares tumbled on Monday following the departure of another high-profile artificial intelligence leader to a rival.
